prompt_text: In the context of structure-based drug design, generate a tight-binding protein binder targeting EGFR (Kinase) expressed by Homo sapiens. The binder should achieve low-nanomolar Kd. specify a protocol for: (1) initial binder scaffold selection using ESMFold, (2) interface design and optimization, (3) specificity profiling. Please include key design considerations and expected challenges.

In the context of structure-based drug design, generate a tight-binding protein binder targeting PDCD1 (Surface antigen) expressed by Homo sapiens. The binder should achieve nanomolar affinity. specify a protocol for: (1) initial binder scaffold selection using AlphaFold2, (2) interface design and optimization, (3) specificity profiling. Please include key design considerations and expected challenges.

In the context of structure-based drug design, generate variants of MMP2 (Metallo protease) from Homo sapiens with altered substrate specificity or improved catalytic efficiency. specify a computational protocol covering: (1) active site scaffold selection, (2) catalytic residue placement using AlphaFold2, (3) substrate binding optimization and variant screening. Describe the key steps and decision points in the workflow.

For a protein engineering research program, optimize the melting temperature (Tm) of KDR (Tyrosine protein kinase VEGFR family) from Homo sapiens. provide a computational approach for: (1) stability hotspot identification, (2) point mutation screening using FoldX or Rosetta, (3) combinatorial variant prioritization. Provide guidance on validation approaches and experimental follow-up.
